results.description: Unit tests colocated in source files

violations.message: Source file 'src\saf\transport_svc.rs' has no colocated #[cfg(test)] module
violations.fix_hint: Add a #[cfg(test)] mod tests { } block to this source file
violations.llm_prompt:
1. Identify the pub/pub(crate) functions in the source file that lack unit tests.
2. For each untested function, add a `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { ... }` block at
   the bottom of the file (or extend an existing one).
3. Inside the tests module, write one `#[test] fn test_<fn_name>_<scenario>()`
   per function under test. The body MUST exercise real behavior and be able
   to FAIL if the function is broken — `assert_eq!`, `assert!`, `assert!(matches!(...))`,
   etc. Per rule 158 (test_not_placeholder), a body that is only `panic!`,
   `todo!`, `unimplemented!`, or empty is NOT acceptable.
4. If a function cannot be tested today (target isn't ready, API in flux),
   leave it untested rather than adding a placeholder. Rule 37 will continue
   to flag it — that's honest feedback, not a trophy.

results.description: Config struct methods must be pub(crate), not pub — configs are not for public consumption

violations.message: `GrpcChannelConfig::new()` is `pub` — Config methods should be `pub(crate)`
violations.fix_hint: Change `pub fn new` to `pub(crate) fn new` in `GrpcChannelConfig`
violations.llm_prompt:
1. For each Config struct method listed in violations:
2. Change `pub fn` to `pub(crate) fn` on all methods of the Config struct
3. If the struct itself is `pub`, consider changing it to `pub(crate)` as well
4. Check that no external crate depends on these methods being public
5. Config structs are internal wiring — they should not be part of the public API
6. Run `cargo check` to verify no external visibility issues

5. Config structs are internal wiring — they should not be part of the public API
6. Run `cargo check` to verify no external visibility issues

results.description: core/ must not import from saf/ — core/ depends only on api/ traits
spi/ must not import from gateway/ — spi/ implements api/ traits only
gateway/ must not import from core/ — gateway/ calls saf/, not core/ directly
saf/ must not import from gateway/ — gateway/ calls saf/, not the reverse
Directory names under src/ must use snake_case
SEA layer files must contain substantive code (not just comments or empty re-exports)
lib.rs must be a real entry point, not a shim with only re-exports
SEA layer mod.rs must not be a crate-level re-export shim
SEA layer directories must meet minimum LOC threshold (api/>10, core/>20, saf/>5)
[lib] and [[bin]] paths must point inside src/
Non-standard [lib] path with src/ present suggests shim directory
Free functions in saf/ or core/ taking an api-defined type as parameter should be methods on that type
Package name must not use SEA layer suffixes as defined in sea_layers config
Workspace root Cargo.toml must not contain [package] — workspace roots must be virtual
